description: Phase LF.3 — per-backend disclosure mode for the web-components context layer. Controls whether the layer pre-loads full component source (`inlineSource: true`) or emits only the catalog index and lets the LLM fetch source on demand (`inlineSource: false`). Claude Code and Codex CLI runs reliably have workdir filesystem access to the catalog symlinks, so they get trim mode. Gemini / Ollama models with weaker tool-call veracity stay in inline mode until their veracity score graduates them. The webComponentsLoader reads this config; the recipe's option value is the default if no per-backend override exists.

graduationCriteria.summary: A backend graduates from `inline` to `trim` mode when ModelQualityScorer.toolCallVeracity (rolling avg over the last 20 miniApp runs) crosses graduationThreshold AND no LF.gate2 markerless-rejection was observed in the same window.
graduationCriteria.demotionTrigger: If a backend in trim mode hits LF.gate2 markerless-rejection 2+ times in any 24h window, automatically demote to inline via DynamicConfigService Firestore hot-patch (no rebuild needed).
graduationCriteria.manualOverride: Operators can pin a backend to either mode via DynamicConfigService key `disclosure.<backend>.<artifactType>.modeOverride = inline | trim`.

miniApp.rationale: Claude Code natively reads files from cwd via the Read tool, and consistently honors marker contracts when given. LF.1 baseline shows trim mode drops chess/ttt prompts from 98KB HARD_WARN to 50KB SOFT_WARN with zero compliance regression.

Codex CLI runs in a seeded workdir with ./web-components and ./examples symlinks, and can read component source on demand. Trim mode keeps selected Lego prompts around the 32KB range instead of preloading 50KB+ of component and golden-assembly source.
Gemini CLI's tool-call surface varies by model. Inline-source mode is safer until per-model veracity stabilizes.
Local Ollama models (qwen-coder, deepseek-coder, etc.) are highly variable on tool-call accuracy. Inline source keeps generation deterministic.
Unknown / unmapped backends default to inline source (safer). Backends explicitly listed in `perBackend` use their listed mode.
